text: Standing at the threshold between corridors and possibility, I am struck by the tranquility that glass and concrete exude. My reflection merges with the distant text, and I catch myself pondering how boundaries—transparent yet unmoving—seem to echo the philosophical barriers between thought and action. The anticipation of entering this R+D space swells inside me. Here, change is implied—development, research, innovation. I feel quietly curious, wondering what realities and imagined futures are being shaped just beyond these glass doors. The soft hum of lighting and the faint echo of distant footsteps remind me of my own presence as an observer and participant—eager to learn, yet momentarily still.
